title: Flaky Spicy Dutch Sourdough Flatbread
description: A light, fermented flatbread with a fiery kick from dried chillies and a fragrant tadka, perfect as a snack or side.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and ground dried chillies.
In a separate bowl, stir the sourdough starter with 200 ml of water until a smooth liquid forms.
Add the wet mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 5–7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rest at room temperature for 1.5 hours, or until it has roughly doubled in size.
After fermentation, punch down the dough, divide it into four equal portions, and shape each into a thin circle about 10 cm in diameter.
Brush each flatbread with a teaspoon of oil and set aside for 5 minutes to relax the gluten.
In a small saucepan, heat a teaspoon of oil over medium heat; add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, and turmeric, and sauté for 30 seconds until the seeds pop and the spices perfume the oil.
Drizzle the hot tadka evenly over the flatbreads, spreading it across the surface.
Preheat the oven to 220 °C (428 °F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Bake the flatbreads for 5–7 minutes, or until golden and slightly puffed.
Remove from the oven, let cool for 2 minutes, then slice and serve warm.
